meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding to overcome no project ideas. An intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Build a modular user onboarding system. By focusing on the critical first-run experience, you solve a universal SaaS problem, create a reusable component for your portfolio, and demonstrate the high-level engineering skills employers demand. It is the ultimate project to showcase technical depth.
content.intro:
The screen stares back, a blinking cursor on an empty IDE. You have the skills, but the paralysis of 'no project ideas' keeps you trapped in tutorial hell. You are waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration, but professional engineers know the truth: ideas are cheap, but execution on core infrastructure is where careers are forged.

Today, we break the cycle. We are building a sophisticated, data-driven user onboarding flow. This isn't just a UI task; it is an exercise in state management, user psychology, and modular architecture. By the end of this guide, you will have a production-ready feature that proves you can build software that actually retains users.

steps.title: Define the State Machine
steps.description: Onboarding is essentially a finite state machine. We define the user journey steps to ensure a predictable flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript state machine definition for a 4-step SaaS onboarding flow including progress tracking and data persistence.

steps.title: Build the Progress Hook
steps.description: We need a custom hook to manage the current step state and persist it to local storage for persistence across refreshes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook 'useOnboarding' that manages step progression and persists state to localStorage.

steps.title: Design the UI Components
steps.description: Create a clean, accessible UI layer that consumes our state machine to show relevant inputs per step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate modular React components for onboarding inputs including a progress bar and navigation buttons.

steps.title: Implement Analytics Triggers
steps.description: Capture user drop-off points by implementing event tracking at every transition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add event tracking middleware to the onboarding state machine to log user progress to a mock analytics endpoint.

steps.title: Stress Test the Flow
steps.description: Use automated testing to ensure the user cannot skip required steps or break the state machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Jest test cases for the onboarding flow, testing edge cases like navigation skips and invalid input.

pro_tips: Always build the 'Skip' path first; it forces you to think about data defaults and optional user states.
Use a single source of truth for step configuration to make your onboarding flow easily extensible.
Never hardcode strings; use a constants file for copy to enable future i18n support.
Implement 'optimistic updates' for your state persistence to make the UI feel instantaneous.
Log every single step completion to a database; this data is pure gold for future optimization.
resources: User Onboarding UX Best Practices: https://www.nngroup.com
content.next_steps: Integrate this onboarding flow into your existing project or use it as the foundation for a new SaaS starter kit.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade component that solves a real business problem. Don't let this knowledge sit idle—push your code, document the architecture, and add it to your portfolio today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
